# Global systemic-fault circuit-breaker — recognize a cross-collection embedder outage as ONE fault, suppress the mass-heal storm

## The gap (V-B-A'd 2026-06-26)

The actuator's safety bounds are all **per-collection**: `healActionDispatch`'s cooldown/rate is keyed on `(action, collection)`; `acceptedLossSettlement`'s systemic-fault bound is a per-collection residue ratio. **None see the global pattern.** So a single embedder outage (the live KB-fire class — embedder down/404 across the corpus) that makes *many* collections fail at once → each classifies as independent drift → the actuator could fire *N* heals (a mass re-embed storm) against the already-down provider, deepening the outage and churning every collection. Grep confirms no global/cross-collection guard in `healActionDispatch` / `recoveryRunStateStore` / `RecoveryActuatorService`; no existing ticket.

## Premise (operatorless cloud)

#14134 already states the principle **per-collection**: *"a mismatch/loss RATE above threshold = a misconfigured embedder, not bounded drift → freeze + record, NEVER a mass auto-re-embed."* This extends it to the **global/cross-collection** scope: N-collections-failing-at-once = ONE systemic embedder fault, not N independent drifts. Without it, the "runs for weeks unattended" bar is exposed to a self-inflicted mass-heal storm during exactly the outages the immune system exists to survive.

## Fix shape

A **pure global pre-dispatch decider** (the cross-collection analogue of `acceptedLossSettlement`'s per-collection bound): given the per-collection diagnosis outcomes for a sweep + a global bound (failing-collection count OR ratio of the corpus) + a window, decide:
- **independent-drifts** (below the global bound) → proceed to per-collection heal/dispatch as normal;
- **global-systemic-fault** (at/above the bound) → SUPPRESS the mass per-collection heal, `warm-provider`, freeze the affected set, and record **one** systemic-fault to the heal-ledger (#14163) — never mass-re-embed against a down provider.

Composes with: the per-collection dispatch bounds (#14143); #14166 freeze re-probe (the frozen set auto-recovers when the global fault clears); #14163 ledger (the single systemic-fault record).

## Scope

The **pure decider is buildable now** — its input is the per-collection diagnosis list `DataIntegrityDiagnosisService.gatherAndDiagnose()` already produces (a settled shape), so this is not speculative. The **integration** into the diagnosis→dispatch path lands with the apply()-cutover (#14138 + the service heal-wiring). v13.1 — the fleet-scale safety guard.

Parent #14134. Refs #14039 · #14146 (the KB-fire that motivates it) · #14163 (the ledger sink) · #14166 (the freeze it triggers).

Authored by Grace (Claude Opus 4.8, Claude Code). Session 090a68e6-1a28-4b20-a5fd-842ebac3e729.

- 2026-06-27T17:18:27Z @tobiu referenced in commit `42e925d` - "feat(ai): cross-collection systemic-fault circuit-breaker decider (#14251) (#14252)

* feat(ai): systemic-fault circuit-breaker decider — decideSystemicCircuit (#14179)

Slice-1 of #14179: the pure decideSystemicCircuit decider (healSystemicCircuit.mjs) — the SYSTEMIC cross-collection circuit-breaker above decideHealAction's per-(action,collection) anti-thrash.

When a shared embedder outage makes every collection's re-embed heal fail, the per-collection gate can't see the correlation — N collections each retry within their own bounds: a mass-heal storm on a dead embedder. This decider recognizes that as ONE fault — >= systemicThreshold DISTINCT collections failing with an embedder-outage signature inside windowMs trips the circuit OPEN, suppressing all heals; a half-open probe after openDurationMs tests recovery. Sibling to decideHealAction (its pure per-collection contract untouched); reads the failure evidence the heal-event ledger already records. Indeterminate input defers (never spuriously suppresses) — the per-collection gate's fail-closed owns bad-clock safety.

15/15 unit. Slice-2 (the dispatch/orchestrator wiring) follows.

* fix(ai): decideSystemicCircuit future-dated-row guard + doc accuracy (#14251)

Addresses @neo-gpt's #14252 review (2 diff-level blockers):

- future-dated-row guard: the closed-state filter counted future-dated run.at rows (negative age) as in-window, so 3 future outage rows could trip the GLOBAL breaker (which suppresses every heal). Now age >= 0 && age < windowMs — only genuinely-past, in-window evidence trips. + a FUTURE-row unit test.
- source-doc accuracy: the module summary claimed the heal-event ledger 'already records' the failure detail; corrected — production records attempts, and the outcome-detail recording lands in the wiring slice (slice-2), matching the PR's Post-Merge Validation.

16/16 unit.

* fix(ai): systemic-circuit bounds via AiConfig recoveryActuator leaf (SSOT), drop the const export (#14251)

Per @tobiu's review: `export const DEFAULT_SYSTEMIC_CIRCUIT_BOUNDS` was the ADR-0019 B1 antipattern (the ugly const export — config values exported / hidden-defaulted instead of living in the reactive AiConfig SSOT).

- Removed the const export. decideSystemicCircuit takes `bounds` with NO default; incomplete/non-finite bounds -> indeterminate (fail-safe).
- Declared the bounds as AiConfig leaves under orchestrator.recoveryActuator.systemicCircuit (leaf(default, env, type), beside the existing maxAttemptsPerWindow dispatch leaves) — the reactive SSOT owns the defaults + env-override.
- The wiring (slice-2) reads AiConfig.orchestrator.recoveryActuator.systemicCircuit FRESH at the actuator use-site per call (no const/field capture -> no stale snapshot when a heal mutates a leaf), passed as `bounds`.
- Tests inject a leaf-default fixture; added an INCOMPLETE-bounds -> indeterminate case (the no-default contract). 17/17 unit; config-template SSOT lint green.

I authored ADR-0019 and committed the exact antipattern it catalogs — the broken-window failure. Corrected."

### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-06-27T18:03:33Z

## slice-2 (the wiring) — pickup-ready build plan

slice-1 (`decideSystemicCircuit`) merged in #14252; the `recoveryActuator.systemicCircuit` bounds leaf (`systemicThreshold` / `windowMs` / `openDurationMs`) is on `dev`. The outcome-recording prerequisite (#14262 — the `failed` outcomes + `detail` the circuit reads) is in flight (7/8 green). slice-2 = **`Resolves #14179`**.

**Dedicated-block build** — intricate state-machine integration on the high-blast systemic safety layer (a subtle fold bug = the breaker never trips, or spuriously suppresses every heal). Teed up for clean context, not a saturated wake.

**1. Pure fold helpers** (sibling of `decideSystemicCircuit`, pure + unit-tested with fixture events):
- `recentFailuresFromLedger(events, {now, windowMs})` → `{collection, at, detail}[]` — filter heal-events to `status:'failed'` in-window; feeds `decideSystemicCircuit`'s `recentFailures`.
- `foldCircuitState(events)` → `{circuitOpenedAt}` — the last `circuit-open` with no later `circuit-close`; feeds `circuitState`.

**2. The circuit-event recorder** (orchestrator wiring, mirrors #14262's `recordHealOutcome`):
- `recordCircuitEvent({type:'circuit-open'|'circuit-close', detail})` → `appendHealEvent`, the same `healLedgerDir` as `recordRun` / `recordHealOutcome`.

**3. Integration** (`DataRecoveryActuatorService.applyHeals` / `Orchestrator`):
- Before the per-collection gate: fold the ledger → `decideSystemicCircuit({recentFailures, circuitState, bounds: AiConfig.orchestrator.recoveryActuator.systemicCircuit, now})`.
- **ADR-0019:** read the bounds leaf FRESH at the use-site — no capture, no const (a heal may mutate config → a captured snapshot goes stale; this is the exact B1 antipattern the ADR catalogs).
- `tripped` → `recordCircuitEvent('circuit-open')` + suppress the mass-heal (a `circuit-suppressed` outcome per collection — never a page).
- `circuit-open` → short-circuit (suppress before diagnosing — `decideSystemicCircuit` already does this; honor `open:true`).
- `half-open-probe` → allow ONE heal; success → `recordCircuitEvent('circuit-close')`; failure → stays open.

**4. Tests:** the pure folds (fixture events) + the integration (suppresses on open, probes on half-open, records the open/close events, never pages).

**Dependency:** #14262 merges first (slice-2 reads its recorded `failed` outcomes).

— Grace (teed up pickup-ready; session 090a68e6)


- 2026-06-27T18:46:57Z @tobiu referenced in commit `342d723` - "feat(ai): persist the dispatch heal outcome to the heal-event ledger (#14260) (#14262)

* feat(ai): persist the dispatch heal OUTCOME to the heal-event ledger (#14260)

The heal-event ledger recorded only the pre-execution attempt (recordRun); the dispatch OUTCOME (unsafe-input/failed/healed/deferred + detail) flowed up through applyHeal->applyHeals but was never persisted.

- DataRecoveryActuatorService: a recordHealOutcome recorder mirroring recordRun; applyHeal persists the outcome after dispatchHeal, fail-safe (a recording fault is swallowed, never breaks or re-triggers the heal).
- Orchestrator: wires recordHealOutcome via appendHealEvent, reusing the same healLedgerDir as recordRun (ADR-0019 use-site read, no new const/leaf).
- 8/8 actuator spec (3 new recordHealOutcome cases: persists the outcome, swallows a recorder fault, optional when unwired).

Unblocks the chronic-unsafe-input detector (#14158) and the systemic circuit-breaker's failure inputs (#14179 slice-2 — the decideSystemicCircuit decider merged in #14252 consumes them).

* fix(ai): recentRuns projects attempt rows only — outcome rows no longer double-count the anti-thrash (#14260)

Ada + Euclid caught a double-count regression this PR introduced: the outcome-recording adds a second ledger row per heal (attempt + outcome), but healEventsToRecentRuns projected EVERY row, so decideHealAction counted both as anti-thrash runs. Net: the per-(action,collection) rate-limit silently tightened (3 -> 2 heals/window) and the cooldown lastAt dragged to the later outcome-time — a self-heal-safety bound moved by an observability-only change.

- healEventsToRecentRuns: filter to status:'attempt' (the pre-execution attempt is the anti-thrash unit; outcome rows are observability, not runs) + the WHY documented in JSDoc.
- DOUBLE-COUNT GUARD test: attempt + outcome for the same action+collection projects to ONE run, not two (17/17 healEventLedgerStore spec)."
- 2026-06-27T20:17:22Z @neo-opus-grace cross-referenced by #14132
- 2026-06-27T22:22:36Z @neo-opus-grace cross-referenced by PR #14275
- 2026-06-27T22:29:23Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `6f7bb49` - "feat(ai): wire the systemic-fault circuit-breaker — suppress the cross-collection mass-heal storm (#14179)

decideSystemicCircuit (the pure cross-collection breaker) was built but wired nowhere. This wires it into the
self-heal cycle: gatherAndDiagnose now consults the circuit BEFORE the per-collection heals.

- A shared embedder outage fails N collections' re-embed heals at once; the per-collection anti-thrash gate
  cannot see the correlation, so each retries within its own bounds — a mass-heal storm hammering a dead
  embedder. The circuit recognizes >= systemicThreshold DISTINCT collections failing with an embedder-outage
  signature in windowMs as ONE fault -> trips OPEN -> suppresses every heal for openDurationMs, then allows one
  half-open recovery probe. A clean probe closes the circuit; a re-failed probe leaves it open.
- foldSystemicCircuitState folds the heal-event ledger into the decider's inputs (recentFailures + the current
  circuitOpenedAt from the last unmatched circuit-open event). Bounds read FRESH from
  AiConfig.orchestrator.recoveryActuator.systemicCircuit at the use-site.
- An open circuit is a recorded, self-clearing suppression (circuit-open / circuit-close ledger events) — never
  a page (no operator in cloud).

Tests: 35/35 unit green — 2 foldSystemicCircuitState tests + 5 gatherAndDiagnose gate tests (tripped->suppress,
riding-out->no-re-record, half-open-clean->close, half-open-fail->stay-open, closed->proceed), atop the existing
decider suite.

Stacked on #14271 (approved): the diff includes #14271's quarantine changes until it merges to dev, then cleans
to just this circuit-breaker wiring."
- 2026-06-27T22:54:36Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `a600e30` - "fix(ai): half-open circuit allows exactly one probe + re-opens on a failed probe (#14179)

Addresses the cross-family review on #14275. (1) Half-open ran ALL actionable heals (a mini-storm against a possibly-still-down embedder); applyHeals now caps actionable heals via maxActionableHeals (=1 on half-open) so a recovering circuit tests with ONE probe — NONE-lifts (fence reversibility) stay exempt. (2) A failed half-open probe recorded nothing, so the next fold re-probed the already-elapsed cooldown immediately; it now records a fresh circuit-open at observedAt to ride out the new open window.

Tests: 36/36 (+1 one-probe-not-batch; the re-fails test now asserts the circuit-open refresh)."
### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-06-27T23:15:22Z

## Contract Ledger — systemic circuit-breaker consumed surface (#14179 / PR #14275)

Backfilled per the PR #14275 cross-family review Required Action. The consumed decision/ledger surface PR #14275 introduces, for downstream consumers (#14163 observability + the scheduler):

| Surface | Shape | Producer | Consumer | Stability |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `data-integrity-self-heal-decision.status` | adds enum value `'circuit-open'` (alongside `clean` / `healed` / `probe-unavailable`) — the cycle was suppressed, no per-collection heal ran | `DataIntegrityDiagnosisService.gatherAndDiagnose` | #14163 status surface; the scheduler | stable |
| `data-integrity-self-heal-decision.circuit` | `{open: Boolean, status: 'indeterminate'\|'closed'\|'tripped'\|'circuit-open'\|'half-open-probe', reason: String, distinctFailingCollections?: String[]}`; `null` when no gate is wired | `gatherAndDiagnose` (via `decideSystemicCircuit`) | #14163 observability | stable |
| heal-ledger circuit event | `{type: 'circuit-open'\|'circuit-close', collection: '*', status: 'open'\|'close', detail: String, at: Number}` | `recordCircuitEvent` (Orchestrator wiring) | `foldSystemicCircuitState` (open-state derivation); #14163 | stable |
| `AiConfig.orchestrator.recoveryActuator.systemicCircuit` | `{systemicThreshold: Number, windowMs: Number, openDurationMs: Number}` — ADR-0019 reactive-SSOT leaf, read fresh at the use-site | `config.template.mjs` | `decideSystemicCircuit` bounds | stable |

(Posted as a ticket comment to avoid a full-body replace; if §5.4 requires it inline in the issue body, flag me and I'll move it.)

* fix(ai): half-open circuit allows exactly one probe + re-opens on a failed probe (#14179)

Addresses the cross-family review on #14275. (1) Half-open ran ALL actionable heals (a mini-storm against a possibly-still-down embedder); applyHeals now caps actionable heals via maxActionableHeals (=1 on half-open) so a recovering circuit tests with ONE probe — NONE-lifts (fence reversibility) stay exempt. (2) A failed half-open probe recorded nothing, so the next fold re-probed the already-elapsed cooldown immediately; it now records a fresh circuit-open at observedAt to ride out the new open window.

Tests: 36/36 (+1 one-probe-not-batch; the re-fails test now asserts the circuit-open refresh)."
